steps.title: Understand what the y-intercept is
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where a graph crosses the y-axis, which always occurs when x = 0.

steps.title: Locate the y-intercept on the graph
steps.content: Tracing from x = 0 on the horizontal axis to where the graph intersects the y-axis, we see the curve passes through the point (0, 5). Therefore, y = 5.
